mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding economic output, which translates into higher corporate earnings and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between INDPRO growth and subsequent 20-day S&P return remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate rising above 5.5% indicates a non-accommodative monetary policy stance that tightens financial conditions and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If after trigger, subsequent 20-day S&P returns are positive in more than 50% of cases over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously low unemployment and high retail sales indicate an overheated economy prompting Federal Reserve tightening, which depresses equity prices over a longer horizon of 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If joint trigger leads to positive forward returns in more than 40% of instances over a 5-year backtest, or if the interaction coefficient in a regression is not statistically significant at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening regime (Fed funds above 5%), a decline in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings and drives S&P 500 lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If falling HOUST in high-rate regime is followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of episodes over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Robust GDP growth reflects strong aggregate demand, which drives corporate profits higher and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: If the correlation between quarter-over-quarter GDP growth and subsequent 60-day S&P returns is non-positive over any 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment signals weakening labor demand and impending recession fears, causing immediate risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If negative payroll months are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a high federal funds rate creates stagflationary conditions, eroding real earnings and forcing valuation compression, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If joint trigger leads to positive 20-day returns in more than 30% of cases over a 2-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate within a month triggers acute risk aversion among market participants, who anticipate consumption slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by 0.2% or more in a single month
failure_condition: If UNRATE jumps are followed by positive weekly S&P returns in more than 40% of cases over a 10-year window.
